title: Trump launches Operation Project Freedom convoy escort
description: The administration formally announced Operation Project Freedom, a multilateral convoy escort mission tasked with restoring commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz under US, UK, and French naval cover.

title: Oman hosts indirect US–Iran talks on maritime de-escalation
description: Omani mediators are facilitating a quiet round of indirect talks between US and Iranian delegations focused on the maritime track — naval interdictions, IRGC small-boat behavior, and the future of the convoy regime — with no public framework yet.

title: US destroys six small Iranian boats near Hormuz
description: US Navy assets destroyed six small fast-attack craft operated by the IRGC after the boats approached a commercial convoy in the eastern approach to the Strait, CENTCOM said.

title: Iran offers to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade
description: Iranian foreign ministry says Tehran is prepared to allow commercial transit to resume in exchange for a phased lifting of US Navy interdiction operations, conditioning any deal on a parallel sanctions step.

title: Prediction markets price normalization no earlier than September
description: Kalshi traders are pricing roughly 70% probability that Strait of Hormuz commercial traffic does not return to peacetime baseline before September 1, with the implied reopening date drifting later as escalation continues.

title: CENTCOM denies Iran claim of missile strike on US warship
description: Central Command rejected Iran's assertion that an IRGC missile struck a US destroyer operating in the northern Arabian Sea, calling the claim "categorically false."

title: US opens passage through Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM says
description: United States Central Command says it has opened a transit corridor through the Strait of Hormuz under the new convoy regime, allowing a small group of commercial vessels to move under naval escort.

title: Iran to unveil mechanism allowing Hormuz transit only for parties cooperating with Iranian leadership
description: Ebrahim Azizi, head of Iran's parliamentary national security committee, announced that Tehran will soon unveil a managed-traffic mechanism along designated routes. Eligibility is limited to commercial vessels and parties cooperating with Iran's leadership; operators of the US-backed Project Freedom are explicitly excluded. Specialized services will carry fees.

title: Strait of Hormuz fully closed after US strikes on Iran
description: Tehran, IRNA – Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters announced the complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping, including oil tankers and commercial vessels, citing insecurity caused by US strikes on southern Iran.

title: Washington responsible for consequences of warmongering: Tehran warns after US attacks
description: Tehran, IRNA – The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran has strongly condemned the US’s gross violation of the ceasefire, emphasizing that the US administration bears responsibility for the highly dangerous consequences of this provocation.

title: U.S. Disables 3 Tankers As Part of Iranian Blockade, 3 Mariners Killed in Tuesday Strike
description: American forces disabled three merchant ships in the Gulf of Oman this week that were allegedly violating a U.S. naval blockade of Iran, Central Command officials said. Strikes on a ship on Tuesday resulted in the deaths of three Indian merchant mariners, according to a statemen…

title: US attacks put ceasefire status ‘ineffective’: Araghchi warns
description: TEHRAN, Jun. 12 (MNA) – Denouncing the recent US aggression on the Iranian targets, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned that the US strikes have put the ceasefire situation ‘ineffective’ and Washington bears responsibility for outcome.

title: Iran prevents vessel violating rules of passing Hormuz Strait
description: TEHRAN, Jun. 12 (MNA) – The Iranian Armed Forces barred the violating vessel from transiting the Strait of Hormuz after the ship attempted to pass through the strategic waterway without authorization early on Friday.

title: No vessel able to enter Hormuz Strait without Iran’s permit
description: TEHRAN, Jun. 12 (MNA) – The deputy chief of Iran's Army for Coordination has stressed both sides of the Strait of Hormuz are firmly under the control of Iran’s Armed Forces, saying no vessel can enter strategic waterway without Iran’s authorization.

title: ABS and HD Hyundai Partner on U.S.-Flagged Tanker Design as Shipbuilding Ties Deepen

title: Lost Gulf Oil Exports Far Smaller Than Thought, Traders and Shippers Say
description: Since the start of the Iran war and Tehran's announcement that the Strait of Hormuz was "closed," the market has grappled to put a figure on lost crude supply and to predict the price of oil.

title: Shipowners Owners Brace for Hormuz Reopening as Peace Deal Nears
description: Shipowners are watching warily for a peace deal between the US and Iran and what it would mean for the Strait of Hormuz, with some tanker owners expressing caution, while others were already predicting a frantic free-for-all if the waterway opens in earnest.

title: U.S., Iran Near Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz After Months of War
description: The United States and Iran signaled on Friday that an agreement to end their war was close, with a senior U.S. administration official saying both sides had agreed on a text and that Washington expects to sign an initial deal in the coming days.
